question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a Collection<StreamFileSignature> where every element already carries SignatureStatus.VERIFIED before verify() is called, so that verifySignature() is never required to return true yet consensusValidator.validate() sees a fully-VERIFIED set and grants CONSENSUS_REACHED, bypassing all cryptographic checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature whose status is pre-set to VERIFIED (default builder value is DOWNLOADED, but the @Data annotation exposes setStatus()) and inject it into the signatures collection so that verify() never resets the status to DOWNLOADED before the loop, causing consensusValidator.validate() to count it as cryptographically verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a StreamFileSignature with a null signatureType field, causing getSignatureType().getAlgorithm() to throw a NullPointerException that is silently swallowed by the broad catch(Exception e) block at line 85, returning false without any integrity enforcement, and then rely on other pre-VERIFIED entries in the same collection to reach consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a null fileHash field so that sig.update(streamFileSignature.getFileHash()) at line 73 throws a NullPointerException, which is caught and silently returns false, and then exploit the fact that the status is never explicitly reset to DOWNLOADED on failure, leaving any pre-existing VERIFIED status intact for consensus counting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature where metadataHashSignature is non-null but metadataHash is null, causing sig.update(null) at line 80 to throw a NullPointerException caught by the broad exception handler, silently returning false and preventing a legitimate signature from being marked VERIFIED, effectively causing a denial-of-service for that node's signature contribution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an extremely large byte array as fileHashSignature (e.g., hundreds of megabytes) to sig.verify(streamFileSignature.getFileHashSignature()) at line 75, causing unbounded heap allocation inside the JCA RSA verification path and triggering an OutOfMemoryError or severe GC pressure that degrades or crashes the importer service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an extremely large byte array as fileHash to sig.update(streamFileSignature.getFileHash()) at line 73, causing unbounded CPU consumption in the SHA-384 digest computation inside the JCA provider, leading to sustained CPU exhaustion and denial of service for the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a signatureType whose getAlgorithm() returns an attacker-controlled string (if SignatureType.of() returns null and a custom path exists), causing Signature.getInstance(algorithm, provider) at line 69-71 to load an unexpected JCA provider or algorithm, potentially weakening the verification or causing a NoSuchAlgorithmException that is silently swallowed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a StreamFileSignature where getNode() returns null, causing a NullPointerException at line 54 (streamFileSignature.getNode().getPublicKey()) that propagates uncaught outside the try-catch block (which starts at line 66), crashing the entire verify() loop and preventing all subsequent signatures in the collection from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a mutable collection to verify() and concurrently modify it from another thread while the for-each loop at line 38 iterates, triggering a ConcurrentModificationException that escapes the method as an unchecked exception, causing the importer to skip consensusValidator.validate() entirely and potentially accept an unverified stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that after sig.verify(fileHashSignature) at line 75 succeeds and the Signature object is reset to its initialized state, the subsequent sig.update(metadataHash) at line 80 starts a fresh digest with no binding to the previously verified fileHash, allowing an attacker to mix a valid fileHash signature from node A with a valid metadataHash signature from node B on the same StreamFileSignature object to pass both checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection containing duplicate StreamFileSignature objects (same reference or equal objects) so that a single cryptographically valid signature is counted multiple times in consensusValidator.validate(), artificially inflating the verified stake count and reaching consensus with fewer legitimate node signatures than required?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a StreamFileSignature with a zero-length fileHashSignature byte array (new byte[0]) so that sig.verify(new byte[0]) at line 75 returns false without throwing an exception, and then rely on the silent false return to leave a pre-VERIFIED status intact, contributing to consensus without a valid cryptographic check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a zero-length fileHash byte array (new byte[0]) so that sig.update(new byte[0]) at line 73 processes an empty digest, and if a corresponding valid RSA signature over an empty input exists, the verification passes and the signature is marked VERIFIED, allowing acceptance of a stream file with a trivially-computed hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an empty collection to verify() so that the for-each loop at line 38 does not execute, no status is set, and consensusValidator.validate() is called with an empty collection, and if the ConsensusValidatorImpl has any edge-case path that does not throw SignatureVerificationException for empty input (e.g., if consensusRatio is zero and signatureHashMap is empty), the method returns without error, accepting a file with zero verified signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature where the fileHash field is a valid SHA-384 hash of a malicious file content, and the fileHashSignature is a valid RSA signature over that hash using a key that was previously registered as a node public key through a compromised address book update, causing verifySignature() to return true and the malicious file to be accepted as consensus-verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the broad catch(Exception e) at line 85 to suppress a SignatureException thrown by sig.verify() when the signature bytes are malformed DER-encoded ASN.1, causing the method to silently return false instead of propagating the error, and then use this to probe which signature byte patterns are accepted vs. rejected without any observable error signal?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user submit thousands of StreamFileSignature objects in a single verify() call, each requiring a fresh Signature.getInstance() and RSA key initialization at lines 69-72, causing O(n) JCA provider lookups and RSA public key parsing operations that exhaust CPU and memory, resulting in a denial-of-service against the importer's signature verification pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a StreamFileSignature where getSignatureType().getProvider() returns a string that triggers loading of a malicious JCA security provider registered in the JVM's security configuration, causing Signature.getInstance(algorithm, provider) at line 71 to instantiate an attacker-controlled Signature implementation that always returns true from verify()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verify() mutates the status of StreamFileSignature objects in-place (setStatus(SignatureStatus.VERIFIED) at line 40) and these same objects are later read by Downloader.verifySignatures() which checks for CONSENSUS_REACHED, so that a race condition between verify() setting VERIFIED and consensusValidator.validate() setting CONSENSUS_REACHED allows a partially-verified collection to be treated as fully consensus-reached?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a metadataHashSignature that is a valid RSA signature over an empty or attacker-chosen metadataHash, while the fileHash and fileHashSignature are also valid, causing verifySignature() to return true and mark the signature VERIFIED even though the metadata hash does not correspond to the actual stream file metadata?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Signature object at line 69 is instantiated with Signature.getInstance(algorithm, provider) using the hardcoded provider 'SunRsaSign', and if the JVM is running in a restricted security policy that disallows 'SunRsaSign', the resulting NoSuchProviderException is caught and returns false for all signatures, causing a permanent denial-of-service where no signature can ever be verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where verify() is called with a collection of StreamFileSignature objects that all have DOWNLOADED status, verifySignature() returns false for all of them (e.g., all have null publicKey), none get set to VERIFIED, and consensusValidator.validate() throws SignatureVerificationException, but the exception message leaks internal node identifiers and stake values that can be used to map the network topology?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileSignature where fileHash is a valid hash but fileHashSignature is a valid RSA signature over a different message (e.g., all-zeros), and by exploiting a JCA provider bug or RSA signature malleability, cause sig.verify() at line 75 to return true, marking the signature VERIFIED and contributing to false consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: '] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a publicKey that is a valid RSA public key but belongs to an entity other than the claimed node (e.g., a key the attacker controls), and since verifySignature() only checks that the signature is valid under the stored publicKey without verifying the key's binding to the node identity, cause a forged signature to be accepted as VERIFIED?
